answers.field_label: How You Heard And Why Success Strategy Session
answers.value:
I first heard about your coaching services through a friend from my local women-in-tech networking group. She had worked with you about a year ago when she was going through a big home downsizing and career transition, and she kept mentioning how much lighter and more focused she felt after addressing her physical and digital clutter. She forwarded me a link to your website and one of your webinars about reclaiming control of your space to support your goals.

Over the last few years, my life has slowly gotten more chaotic and cluttered, both at home and in my work environment. I started working remotely full-time during the pandemic, and my dining room became my office almost overnight. I never really set it up intentionally; I just kept adding more piles, more supplies, and more "temporary" storage solutions that somehow became permanent. I’m now at the point where I have:

- Two bookshelves overflowing with binders, old notebooks, and reference materials I almost never use
- Stacks of unopened mail and "to file" paperwork on the end of the dining table and on a sideboard
- Three cardboard boxes in the corner that I moved from my last apartment three years ago and still haven’t fully unpacked
- Office supplies in multiple places, so I keep buying duplicates because I can’t find what I already own
- A laptop and second monitor on the table, surrounded by random cords, sticky notes, and half-finished projects

Beyond the physical clutter, I’m also dealing with a lot of mental and digital clutter. I juggle multiple software projects at work, and my email inbox is out of control. I have thousands of unread messages, old project threads I haven’t archived, and I rely on my memory and scattered to-do lists rather than a clear, trusted system. I often feel like I’m reacting all day instead of proactively planning, which leads to long evenings trying to "catch up" and feeling guilty that I’m not more organized.

The main reasons I’m seeking a Success Strategy Session are:

1. **I want to reclaim my home office/dining area so it feels calm and functional instead of stressful.** Right now, I avoid inviting friends over for dinner because I’m embarrassed about the mess. I’d love to be able to close my laptop at the end of the day and transition the space back to an actual dining room, instead of always feeling like I’m sitting next to a pile of unfinished work.

2. **I’m struggling with focus and follow-through.** I start my days with good intentions, but as soon as I sit down, I get visually overwhelmed by everything around me. I’ll start responding to an email, then see a sticky note reminding me of another task, then notice a bill in a pile, and suddenly I’m jumping between five different things and not finishing any of them. I suspect my environment is feeding this scattered energy, and I’d like help creating a system that supports deep focus and clear priorities.

3. **I feel like clutter is costing me time, money, and peace of mind.** I’ve paid late fees because I mislaid bills under other papers. I’ve bought items I already own (like printer ink and notebooks) because I couldn’t find them. I waste time every week looking for documents, cords, or notes I know I wrote "somewhere." It’s not just inconvenient anymore; it feels like it’s holding me back professionally and personally.

4. **I’m at a transition point and want my environment to reflect where I’m going, not where I’ve been.** I recently got promoted to Senior Project Coordinator, and I’m seriously considering moving into a project management role in the next year or two. I want my systems, both physical and digital, to match that next level of responsibility. Right now, my workspace still feels like a temporary setup from my early pandemic days, instead of a stable, intentional environment for the career I’m building.

5. **I’ve tried to tackle this on my own, but I get stuck and overwhelmed.** I’ve watched organizing videos, read blogs, and even started a few decluttering challenges. I usually make some progress for a weekend, then I stall out when I hit decisions that feel emotional (like what to do with old journals, work notes, or sentimental items from my late grandmother). I also struggle to create sustainable systems, so even when I tidy up, things slowly drift back to chaos within a few weeks.

I’m hoping that in a Success Strategy Session with you, we can:

- Identify the biggest sources of friction in my home office/dining area and prioritize what to address first
- Create a simple, realistic plan to reduce physical and paper clutter that fits with my limited time and energy
- Develop a few concrete habits or routines (daily and weekly) so I can maintain any progress I make
- Talk through some of the emotional side of letting go of certain items, especially old work materials and sentimental papers
- Begin to design a workspace that supports focused, high-quality work during the day and allows me to mentally "shut off" in the evenings

Overall, I’m not looking for a Pinterest-perfect home. I just want to feel in control of my environment instead of feeling like my environment is controlling me. I’d like to be able to sit down at my desk in the morning and feel clear about what I’m doing, and then close my laptop at night without feeling haunted by all the piles around me. I’m hoping your structured approach and outside perspective will help me finally break this pattern of clutter and overwhelm and replace it with systems that actually work for how my brain and my life function in reality.
